programmes.show_description: When Dreams Come True: Fun and drama with the Yorkshire vets patrolling the livestock in the 1930s. Helen is finally up and about, but chaos is just around the corner.
Poetic Justice: Comedy with the Chigwell sisters and their man-mad neighbour. On a prison visit to the Isle of Wight, Dorien finally meets Chris.
The Place I Called My Home: In the wake of national tragedy, Corcoran reels along with the rest of the country. Corcoran, Morehouse and Doctor Freeman join a national effort.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Flavour of the Month: Another slice of Chigwell life with the Essex sisters. Tracey's good deed backfires when she tries to help an ex-con.
A Night to Forget: Quinnan's stag night is being organised by Hollis, so will anything go right? The evening certainly promises a few surprises!
New. Cat Fight: The campus surgery remains closed while inquiries into the mysterious death of the former campus doctor continue and Julia clashes with Eva over the delay.
New. Friends Like These: Daniel helps a recovering alcoholic to get his life back on track and has a revelation about his own behaviour in the process.
We'll Meet Again: Diane faces a personal crisis that jeopardises her relationship with Owen, while Ric has to deal with the repercussions of his night with Connie.
The Enemy Within: Charlie offers emotional support to Ruth after she discovers a devastating secret about her husband Edward, but the move comes at a huge cost to himself.
A Night to Forget: Quinnan's stag night is being organised by Hollis, so will anything go right? The evening certainly promises a few surprises!
New. Lucy admits the truth to Ian, and Ricky feels Carol's wrath. Ben's party goes off with a bang when Shirley gets the wrong end of the stick, and an unexpected visitor appears.
New. Lucy puts Ian in an impossible situation, while Phil has something to hide. Jean's behaviour causes concern for Max - but it looks like the worst is yet to come.
A New Chapter: Nostalgic fun at the 1930s veterinary practice. James and Helen are busy preparing for their move to Rowangarth and more hospitality from Granville Bennett.
Leo and Orrin engage in a violent argument over Abby, causing them both to fall in the river. Meanwhile, Tom is furious with Jack for neglecting his work.
If It Be Man's Work: Frank and Lu are drawn into Stratford's Silicon Valley when Spider asks for their help to expose a mole in a tech company.
Mice: Comedy with Chigwell's most famous sisters. Tracey's plan to hold a perfume-selling party hits a major snag when Sharon discovers some furry gate-crashers.
Who's That Looking Sideways at Nelly?: Roy Clarke's long-running sitcom, here in its 30th series. 'Undercover agent' Hobbo finds himself the new recipient of Marina's affections.
Nobody Messes with Tony the Throat: More tips on growing old disgracefully! Howard is concerned when Pearl starts going out every day with Nelly, and she will not say where.
Will Stella Find True Love with Norris Fairburn?: Popular sitcom, still young at heart in its 30th series! Serial groom Norris is free again - surely an ideal husband for Stella.
Walking with Ghosts: Halloween gets spookier when a ghost tour guide sees a body being buried on the beach, but when police uncover nothing, Pearl is on the lookout for a missing body.
Someone Like Me: A taxi driver turns up dead with a bullet through his head, and an investment banker is nearly assassinated.
Congratulations: Another mysterious case for the experienced detectives. Pullman struggles to keep the boys in line as their chequered pasts begin to catch up with them.
A Traitor to Memory: Crime-cracking adventures with Nathaniel Parker as the aristocratic detective. Is a violinist connected to the death of a woman in a hit-and-run incident?
A New Chapter: Nostalgic fun at the 1930s veterinary practice. James and Helen are busy preparing for their move to Rowangarth and more hospitality from Granville Bennett.
From the Cradle to the Grave: Chaos ensues when a museum's Egyptian mummy case turns out to contain the body of a very recently deceased woman.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Cuckoo: Comedy from darkest Essex - Chigwell to be precise. Sharon gets a shock when she tries to collect her unemployment giro.
A Day to Remember: What could possibly go wrong on Quinnan's wedding day? Garfield's unexpected reappearance is only the beginning!
New. Hearts Will Go On: Melody helps a lecturer and his student who are willing to sacrifice their careers to protect their illicit romance, and Jimmi declares his love for Eva.
New. Hush Little Baby: Ruth helps a pregnant girl stand up to the father of her baby. Vivien experiences problems with public transport, while Eva considers moving back in with Jimmi.
Holding On: Rosie receives good news, but Mubbs does not seem interested, and Diane struggles to cope as Joanna's life hangs in the balance - along with that of her unborn baby.
Guilty Secrets: Ruth stands by her decision to move Charlie, but gets a frosty reception on her first day as clinical standards manager.
A Day to Remember: What could possibly go wrong on Quinnan's wedding day? Garfield's unexpected reappearance is only the beginning!
New. Max is determined to find Stacey, but his grim task leaves him shaken. Becca offers comfort, but does she know more than she's letting on?
New. When Max finally tracks down Stacey, old wounds are re-opened and fresh ones created.
A Present from Dublin: Christopher Timothy talks to the animals once more. Calum welcomes the new arrivals - one that bites and one that fits Siegfried's domestic requirements.
Tom is attacked by an intruder at the boatyard, while Gerald and Polly have a furious row and end their marriage.
If Music Be the Food of Love: Frank and Lu enter the competitive world of ballroom dancing and learn the dangers of falling for your dance partner.
Brief Encounter: More laughs with the Essex sisters. A beautiful friendship begins for Sharon when she sees off a mugger who is attacking a businessman in Chigwell railway station.
Will Randolph Make a Good Impression?: Another episode of senior silliness. Hobbo arrives at the river wall with Randolph, who he hopes will bring romance into Stella's life.
In Which Romance Springs a Leak: Roy Clarke's long-running sitcom, still with a spring in its step! Toby realises that going back to his ex-wife may have its benefits.
Variations on a Theme of Father's Day: Young-at-heart sitcom. Eager to find out more about his supposed mother Nelly, Hobbo asks Howard to eavesdrop on her when she's visiting Pearl.
Murder in the Dark: Part One: When a man is killed at her university reunion, Becks finds herself on the wrong side of an investigation. Was a friend responsible or did someone break in?
Murder in the Dark: Part Two: As Judith and Suzie work with the police to find the killer, tensions rise among Becks' friends in the house. Guest starring Harry Enfield.
Casualty: An involving mystery for the veteran detectives including James Bolam and Dennis Waterman. Jack decides to take revenge against his wife's killer by running him down.
A Cry for Justice: Nathaniel Parker stars in the gripping murder mystery series from the mind of Elizabeth George. A murder in Oxford takes Lynley away from home and his wife.
